title: ODS Policy Input v1
description: The structured object ODS feeds to OPA Rego policy evaluation at the check stage. All fields are set by the ODS CLI pipeline (detect → analyze → score) and are available as `input.*` inside your Rego policy.

ai_generated.description: Whether AI code was detected in the diff.

ai_confidence.description: Aggregate AI detection confidence (0.0–1.0). 0 means no signal; 1.0 means multiple high-confidence signals.

detection_sources.description: Which detection signals fired. Lets policies separate disclosed AI use (author attribution: commit-trailer, git-ai-notes, pr-body) from merely suspected AI use (branch-name, diff-heuristics only), making author-disclosure norms (SFC guidance, kernel docs) checkable at the gate.

technical_debt_delta.description: Weighted technical-debt impact of the PR. Higher positive = more debt added. Negative values indicate debt reduction.

test_coverage.description: Line coverage rate (0.0–1.0). -1 means not measured (no coverage file found). Policies should guard with `input.test_coverage >= 0` before comparing.

test_coverage_source.description: How test_coverage was obtained. 'unknown' means it was not measured.

branch.description: The PR head branch name (e.g. claude/add-auth, feature/oauth).

changed_files.description: Paths of all files changed in the PR (all types, not just code files).

issues.description: Quality issues found by ods analyze. Each item represents one finding from the heuristic analyzer or an ingested SARIF report.

ai_files.description: Files detected as containing AI-generated code by ods detect.

ai_reviews.description: AI code-reviewer verdicts supplied via `ods check --ai-review` (see schemas/review-verdict/v1.json). Kept separate from `issues` on purpose: deterministic findings may deny, probabilistic reviewer opinions default to routing attention only. A policy must reference this section explicitly to let LLM findings block.

rule.description: Rule identifier. Built-in rules are prefixed ai- (e.g. ai-unsafe-deserialization). SARIF rules use the tool's own id.

line.description: Line number (0 when the location is not known).

ai_lines.description: Estimated number of AI-generated lines in the file.

total_lines.description: Total lines added in this file.

confidence.description: AI detection confidence for this file (0.0–1.0).

model.description: Model identifier used for the review, when known.

verdict.description: The reviewer's overall conclusion. Only verdicts matching the evaluated HEAD enter the input — stale verdicts are skipped upstream.

category.description: Free-form finding class, e.g. correctness, design, security.
